match_info.result: Rajasthan Royals won by 8 wickets (with 47 balls remaining)

volatility_index.interpretation: One-sided — RR dominated the chase from the first ball

turning_points.label: The Powerplay Demolition
turning_points.description: Archer and Burger dismantled CSK's top order with clinical precision. Samson fell for 6, Gaikwad for 6, Mhatre for 0 — three wickets by over 3. Short followed in over 5. By the end of 6 overs, CSK had already lost their batting blueprint.
turning_points.win_prob_shift: CSK's win probability never exceeded 30% after this.

turning_points.label: Jadeja's Double Strike (Overs 7-8)
turning_points.description: Sarfaraz (17) and Dube (6) fell within the same over to Ravindra Jadeja — 51/5 became 57/6. The partnership was snuffed out. Suddenly CSK were looking at a total well under 120.
turning_points.win_prob_shift: CSK win probability collapsed to under 10%.

turning_points.label: Sooryavanshi's Blitzkrieg (Overs 1-6)
turning_points.description: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, 14 years old, smashed 52 from 17 balls. Five sixes, four fours. He attacked every CSK bowler but reserved special punishment for Matt Henry (who conceded 40 in 3 overs). By the time Kamboj removed him, RR needed just 53 from 81 balls with 9 wickets in hand.
turning_points.win_prob_shift: RR win probability was over 90% from ball 10 of the chase.

counterfactual_scenarios.title: What If CSK's Top Order Had Held?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: Gaikwad scores 40 instead of 6; Mhatre avoids the Burger bouncer to make 15
counterfactual_scenarios.original_outcome: RR won by 8 wickets (128 chased in 12.1 overs)
counterfactual_scenarios.lola_outcome: CSK reach 170-175. Chase becomes a genuine contest.
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative:
Imagine over 3 differently. Instead of Mhatre nicking off Burger's bouncer for a first-ball duck, he reads the length and pulls it through mid-wicket for four. Gaikwad — who looked to make room and was caught plumb in front — instead works the ball through leg and builds a 40-ball 40. The top order gives CSK's middle order something to chase.

With Sarfaraz and Kartik Sharma joining at 75/2 rather than 38/4, the dynamic changes. Sarfaraz's 17 off 12 would have come in a different context — not as a rescuer but as an accelerator. Dube gets more balls. CSK scratch to 170-175.

RR chasing 175 means Sooryavanshi's blitz still puts them in front, but when he falls at 75 in over 6, the asking rate is now 13-14 an over from over 7. Jaiswal must do it largely alone. The game, suddenly, has a pulse.

counterfactual_scenarios.title: What If Matt Henry Had Been Parsimonious?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: Henry bowls at 8 RPO (his career T20 average) instead of 13.33 RPO — saving 16 runs in his 3 overs
counterfactual_scenarios.lola_outcome: RR still win, but needing 8 more balls — win by 6 wickets with 38 balls remaining instead
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative:
Henry was CSK's most expensive bowler — 40 runs from 3 overs, an economy of 13.33 in a match where the team totalled 127. Three of Sooryavanshi's sixes came off Henry's bowling. If Henry had bowled at 8 RPO — decent but not threatening — those 16 saved runs make the chase target effectively 144.

In this world, Sooryavanshi's power still removes the early pressure but doesn't quite break the match open as comprehensively. Jaiswal, who scored 38 from 36 balls in the actual match, might need to shift gears slightly earlier. Jurel's cameo of 18 from 9 becomes load-bearing rather than decorative.

But make no mistake: RR still win. Chasing 144 with Sooryavanshi's early 52 still leaves the job very manageable. The real lesson here isn't that Henry cost CSK the match — CSK were always going to lose this. The lesson is about the margin: a sub-100 total after a horror powerplay collapse doesn't leave room for any bowler to be expensive.

commentary_gems.text: Burger fires one at 140kph, and Samson is beaten after looking to heave across the line. On a length on middle, and the ball seams away after pitching. Samson went swinging without any foot movement, and missed. His first innings in yellow, against the side he made a name with, is cut short early.

Sooryavanshi vs Henry from ball one. Short of a length around fifth stump — Sooryavanshi shifted his weight back to slash it, misfield at point by Sarfaraz, and that allows him two. The tone is set immediately.

Archer kept the ball fullish and straight, and Gaikwad, looking to open the face of the bat and perhaps slice it, missed. The stumps lit up. There was the swag on Archer's face after launching that ball at 143kph.
Royals win with nearly eight overs to spare. They get there with a gentle tuck off the pads to midwicket. Sedate celebrations.
No venom on the bouncer, landed very short but didn't rise much. Overton made room and pulled it, almost got a whiplash as the ball sailed over wide long-on.

game_changers.name: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
game_changers.reason: Sooryavanshi didn't just win the match — he made it a non-event within the first six overs. At 14, he became the youngest player to score a half-century in IPL history, dispatching Matt Henry for three sixes in a single over. His blitz turned a chaseable 128 into a lap of honour.

game_changers.name: Jofra Archer
game_changers.reason: Archer's opening spell was scalpel-precise. He bowled Gaikwad with a delivery that took the off stump at 143kph, and dismissed Noor Ahmad later to leave CSK's innings with no foundation to build from. Seventeen dot balls in four overs in T20 cricket is exceptional — Archer made the powerplay feel like a Test match.

game_changers.name: Jamie Overton
game_changers.reason: CSK were 82 for 8 — mathematically in the match but aesthetically dead. Overton refused to capitulate. His 43 from 36 added 45 runs for the 9th wicket with Noor Ahmad (who contributed 1) and continued beyond, ultimately taking CSK past 120. Without Overton, CSK might not have reached 100.

game_changers.name: Nandre Burger
game_changers.reason: Burger's opening over was the first nail. He clean-bowled Samson with an outswinger that beat the drive, then — on a hat-trick — removed Mhatre via a DRS review when the ball grazed the glove. Both dismissals came in the powerplay and fractured CSK's batting architecture before it was built.
